opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

capacity.description: The storage capacity of the drive in GB (e.g., 4000 GB)

storage_type.description: The type of storage drive (either HDD or SSD)

form_factor.description: The physical size and shape of the drive (e.g., M.2-2280, 2.5", 3.5")

interface.description: The interface used by the drive (e.g., M.2 PCIe 4.0 X4, SATA 6Gb/s)

cache.description: The cache size of the drive in MB.

nvme.description: Whether the drive uses the NVMe protocol. Only applicable to SSDs

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

series.description:
Product series name

The series is the manufacturer's marketed product line / family for the drive. Manufacturer (e.g., Samsung, Western Digital, Crucial, Seagate, Kingston, Sabrent, SK hynix, Lexar) is stored separately, as are capacity (`capacity`), form factor (`form_factor`: M.2-2280, 2.5", 3.5"), interface (`interface`: PCIe 5.0 x4, SATA 6Gb/s), storage_type (SSD vs HDD), and `nvme`. So do NOT repeat any of those in the series. Capacity (1TB, 2TB, 4TB) is NEVER part of the series. Examples: 'Samsung 990 Pro 2TB M.2-2280 PCIe 4.0 X4 NVMe' → series='990 Pro'. 'Samsung 870 EVO 1TB 2.5" SATA' → series='870 EVO'. 'WD_BLACK SN850X 2TB' → series='WD_BLACK SN850X' (the color tier is part of WD's marketed family name and IS part of the series for WD). 'WD_BLUE SN580 1TB' → series='WD_BLUE SN580'. 'WD Red Plus 4TB 3.5"' → series='WD_RED Plus'. 'Crucial T705 2TB Heatsink' → series='T705'. 'Crucial MX500 1TB 2.5" SATA' → series='MX500'. 'Seagate BarraCuda 2TB 3.5" 7200RPM' → series='BarraCuda'. 'Seagate FireCuda 530 1TB' → series='FireCuda 530'. 'Lexar NM790 2TB' → series='NM790'. The series is what stays consistent across capacity tiers of the same product line.

variant.description:
Specific variant in the series

Most drives should have an empty variant — capacity, form factor, interface, NVMe, and storage type are all stored in their own fields. Only fill `variant` when the manufacturer ships a genuinely distinct sub-model under the same series name that needs its own selector. The most common reason in practice is a heatsink-bundled SKU vs the bare-drive SKU of the same series + capacity. Examples: 'Samsung 990 Pro 2TB' (no heatsink) → variant=''. 'Samsung 990 Pro 2TB with Heatsink' → variant='Heatsink'. 'Crucial T705 2TB' → variant=''. 'Crucial T705 2TB Heatsink' → variant='Heatsink'. 'WD_BLACK SN850X 2TB' (bare) → variant=''. 'WD_BLACK SN850X 2TB Heatsink' → variant='Heatsink'. 'Seagate FireCuda 530 1TB Heatsink (PS5)' → variant='Heatsink'. The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, series, capacity, form_factor, interface) group; if every drive in a group ends up with the same value, leave it empty.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
